section_miscategorizations.text: possible breast cancer given family history
section_miscategorizations.reason: Family history itself is a subjective/history element; while the clinician is using it to frame a possible diagnosis, the wording emphasizes family history rather than a synthesized assessment based on objective findings. This is a borderline case and does not substantially mislead the reader.

section_miscategorizations.text: Counsel the patient on increasing fluid intake and slow positional changes to manage orthostatic symptoms.
section_miscategorizations.reason: The plan references orthostatic symptoms that are not documented in S or O. The counseling action belongs in P, but the undocumented symptom reference would more appropriately be introduced in S (as patient-reported) or A (as part of the assessment) before appearing in the plan. This is an organizational issue rather than a clear section-type error.
